entry.title: Efficient Management of CBC Type Transport Requests in S/4HANA Cloud Public Cloud
entry.content:
What is a Transport Request?

A transport request is a mechanism in SAP that facilitates the movement of changes made in one system to another. In the case of CBC, transport requests are automatically generated when deploying a workspace. These requests document all modifications, allowing for a structured approach to change management.

Identifying CBC Transport Requests

When you deploy your workspace from CBC, the system creates transport requests categorized as CBC type. These requests capture essential changes, including:

  • Scoping adjustments
  • Organizational unit modifications
  • Business configuration content updates

Finding CBC Transport Requests

To locate and manage these transport requests, follow these steps:

  1. Access the Export Customizing Transports App: This app provides a centralized interface for managing transport requests.

  2. Filter by Request Type: Use the "Type" option to specifically filter for requests labeled as "SAP Central Business Configuration." This will help you focus on relevant transport requests.

  3. Check Task Status: Ensure that all tasks associated with the CBC requests have a "released" status. Only requests in this state can be processed further.

Handling CBC Transport Requests

Taking Ownership of a Request

To manage a CBC transport request, you can take ownership by clicking the Assign To Me button. This grants you the necessary permissions to release the request once all associated tasks are complete.

Releasing Customizing Requests

You can release customizing requests using the Export Customizing Transports app. It’s important to note that all tasks within requests of type CBC are automatically released after implementation, ensuring a smooth transition of changes.

Monitoring CBC Staging Status

Transport requests of type Central Business Configuration are linked to a CBC Staging ID. To check the staging status and gather related information, select Display CBC Staging in the transport request overview. This will direct you to the CBC staging area, providing more detailed insights.

If you encounter issues during staging, such as errors or activation failures, it’s essential to report an incident under the component X4-CBC-CW for assistance.

Important Notes

  • No Task Creation: For CBC type requests, task creation is not permitted.
  • Release Requirement: All tasks within a CBC transport request must have a "released" status before proceeding. If any tasks are not released, please report an incident under X4-CBC-CW.

entry.title: Localization and Identity & Access Management (IAM) with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408
entry.content:
Hello and welcome to this blog covering the release highlights for localization and Identity & Access Management (IAM) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408. With this release, we deliver a new local version for public sector and additional features for SAP-delivered local versions. In addition, we offer an improved access to documentation for Identity and Access Management (IAM).

Topics covered in this blog:

Localization

  • Local Version for Public Sector
  • Enhancements for Existing SAP-Delivered Versions

Identity and Access Management (IAM)

  • Unified Access to Documentation About Identity and Access Management on SAP Help Portal

 

Localization

Local Version for Public Sector

With the new release, we add a new local industry version for Public Sector including government budget and grants management, fund accounting, and core scope items for finance, procurement, logistics, asset accounting, treasury, leasing, sales, plant maintenance, contract account, and project management.

Value Proposition

Support of public sector users with public cloud solution specific for Germany, Canada, Australia, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.

Capabilities

The new scope items in solution scenario SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Public Sector are 6YU, 7AU, 71S, 78S, 6UC, 6R3, 6X0.

Note

For Germany and Canada, an initial version was already delivered with release 2402. New scope items as per above are planned to be added.

For the US all customers not needing GASB accounting principles should also use this version with 2408. These customers shall not use the separate Public Sector edition for the US SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud for public sector any more.

 

Enhancements of Existing Local Versions

Local Versions

Local versions help you meet local requirements by taking into account diverse legal frameworks, and different languages, and by complying with a wide range of local financial reporting standards, tax calculations and laws.

​Value Proposition​

  • Steer business operations globally with ease utilizing SAP-delivered localizations​
  • Address increasing legal changes and compliance requirements on time​
  • Leverage standard processes and automation to address regulatory requirements worldwide, like taxes, e-invoicing, statutory reporting, and payments
New Capabilities for Existing SAP-Delivered Local Versions

Chile

  • Purchase register
  • Sales register

Greece

  • Accounting documents report / document journals

Japan

  • Enhancement of the monthly invoice printing form
  • VAT return to include contract accounting documents

Oman

  • Withholding tax declaration

Peru

  • Inventory and balance ledger for statutory reporting

Serbia

  • E-invoicing for incoming invoices
  • Journal entries audit files

Singapore

  • Payment items approval
  • Cash flow statement

Slovenia

  • Acquisition tax accrual solution
  • BST, KRD and SFT reports
  • Deferred tax

Spain

  • Enablement of SEPA formats for 2024

Switzerland

  • Implementation of a dunning letter
  • Cash flow statement

Ukraine

  • Balance sheet, profit and loss, and cash flow statements
  • Output print forms for logistics

United Kingdom

  • Direct-debit synchronization, notification of mandate changes, and SAP Fiori app for mandate
  • Intercompany settlement in convergent invoicing

 

Identity and Access Management (IAM)

Unified Access to Documentation About Identity and Access Management on SAP Help Portal

Unlock the full potential of SAP S/4HANA Cloud with the unified access to documentation about Identity and Access Management on SAP Help Portal. With user and identity management, roles and authorization, and authentication, you can control and specify user access to apps, ensuring regulatory compliance and security standards. Familiarize yourself with the key concepts and apps to streamline implementation along the different phases and maximize efficiency.

 

Value Proposition 

  • One-stop shop for identity and access management (IAM) documentation
  • Documentation of tasks related to IAM for the different implementation phases (prepare, realize, run, and upgrade) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and integrated products
  • Overview of apps for user and identity management, roles and authorization, and authentication

 

Capabilities 

  • Support users in each implementation phase with dedicated IAM documentation
  • Provide a holistic view on IAM for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and integrated products from initial system access to tasks that need to be completed before, during, and after the upgrade of SAP S/4HANA Cloud solutions
  • Gain an overview of different identity management scenarios

For more information, see Identity and Access Management

 

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

 

️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a recording and a presentation for GRC, Trade and Tax as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime. 

 

Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.2
entry.content:
Preliminary versions of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.2.

You can find this preview on the current Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.2”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.2 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

In this release, try the new Commenting Feature for What's New documentation. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: Two Tier ERP Transactional Data Replication options
entry.content:
Two Tier ERP Integration:

Integration is crucial for Two-Tier ERP scenarios, as understanding end-to-end business processes, including both master and transactional data flows, is vital for customer success. It’s important to identify integration requirements early in the process. SAP S/4HANA Cloud will also need to integrate with various other solutions within the customer’s system landscape. Two-Tier ERP offers multiple deployment options, For instance, let's look into one of the deployement option where companies running their main ERP systems (headquarters) on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition or SAP S/4HANA on-premise can connect to subsidiaries using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.

Two Tier ERP Transactional Data Integration options

For easier understanding, we have separated integration into two categories: Master Data and Transactional Data. This blog focuses on the options for transactional data integration, while you can find the master data options in this linked blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/two-tier-erp-master-data-replication-options/ba-p/13879970

Option 1: BTP Integration Suite - Cloud Integration

Top 5 reasons to choose SAP Integration Suite

  1. Tackle ANY integration challenge with a holistic approach.
  2. Accelerate business outcomes with out-of-the-box integration.
  3. Innovate faster with an open API platform for the future.
  4. Speed time to value.
  5. Outperform with enterprise-grade integration.

The Integration Suite is applicable in the following Two-Tier use cases:

  • Headquarters is using S/4HANA on-premise, S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, while subsidiaries are on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  • The customer has a mixed landscape, including non-SAP solutions that need to integrate with headquarters.
  • The customer is currently using SAP S/4HANA Cloud editions and plans to expand to additional applications in the future.
  • The customer already has a Business Technology Platform (BTP) account with the Integration Suite.

Key Aspects of SAP Integration Suite from a Two-Tier ERP Integration Perspective:

  • SAP Cloud Integration enables seamless connections between both cloud and on-premise systems, whether they are SAP or non-SAP.
  • It supports real-time scenarios and provides a wide range of pre-built integration content (Integration Packages), along with extensive capabilities and connectivity options.
  • Integration Flows (iFlows) allow you to design message processing logic and configure sender/receiver adapters within SAP Cloud Integration.

You can find details of one of the Two-Tier ERP Buyer and Supplier scenario integration flow here https://api.sap.com/package/IntegrationofSAPS4HANACloudProcurementwithOnPremiseSuppliersS4HANAorERP/integrationflow

Option 2: SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Don’t have an SAP BTP account with the Integration Suite service? No worries—you can still meet your integration needs through the API Hub.

  • Seamlessly discover content that addresses all your integration and extension requirements.
  • Explore integrations between SAP products as well as between SAP and third-party applications.
  • Browse various products, categories, business processes, partners, featured APIs, integrations, and more here https://api.sap.com/search?searchterm=Two%20Tier&tab=All&$refinedBy=true

The key takeaway of this blog is our recommendation to use the SAP Integration Suite service for transactional data replication wherever applicable, allowing you to leverage pre-built integration content for your scenarios.

Future Exploration
In upcoming blogs, we will explore Master Data and Transactional Data in greater detail, and discuss how they can benefit your business.

entry.title: Seamless Integration: Connecting Sales Cloud V2 Opportunity Management with S/4HANA Customer Project
entry.content:
Story:

Prasad, a Sales Manager at PK Solutions, was excited about closing a big opportunity using SAP Sales Cloud V2. However, once the deal was won, handing over the project details to her colleague David, a Project Manager using S/4HANA Customer Projects, became cumbersome and prone to miscommunication. Prasad's sales data wasn’t seamlessly transferring to David’s project system, causing delays in project planning. After integrating Sales Cloud V2 with S/4HANA, Prasad could effortlessly sync opportunity details, giving David real-time visibility. Now, they collaborate smoothly, ensuring projects kick off faster with no data gaps, leading to satisfied customers and more efficient workflows

S/4HANA Customer Project in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud

As a rapidly expanding company, PK Solutions struggled to bridge the gap between sales opportunities and project execution. While their sales team leveraged SAP Sales Cloud V2 to manage opportunities, project managers depended on S/4HANA Customer Projects for delivery, leading to communication breakdowns and delays due to the lack of integration. By connecting Sales Cloud V2 with S/4HANA, they streamlined the entire opportunity-to-project process, enabling real-time visibility and smoother handovers. This seamless integration enhanced team collaboration, driving operational efficiency and boosting customer satisfaction.

Architecture diagram 

Process Flow: 

  • Opportunity created in Sales Cloud (v2) 
  • Opportunity set to In Process ----> Triggers autoflow call to create customer project via Integration Suite 
  • Customer project created with status ‘In Planning’ in S/4HANA Cloud via the integration 

Sales Cloud v2 – configurations : 

Step 1: Please replicate the master data from S/4HANA to Sales Cloud V2

Step 2 : Create a new Opportunity Type or use an existing one

Step 3: Create an iFlow that generates a customer project and triggers the auto-flow based on your specified conditions.

Step 4: Create an iFlow that generates a customer project and triggers the auto-flow based on your specified conditions.

Step 5: Create an Opportunity with below data

Step 6: Set the status= In process 

Step 7: Project ID will back V2 from S/4HANA

Conclusion

With the integration of Sales Cloud V2 and S/4HANA Customer Projects, Prasad and David eliminated workflow inefficiencies, leading to faster project execution and improved collaboration. This seamless connection ultimately enhanced customer satisfaction and business success.

entry.title: RAP based Fiori application implemented with Custom Entities - License Compliance Digital AccessV2.0
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

In this post, I wanted to share my knowledge and experience about “A RAP based Fiori application implemented with Custom Entities called License Compliance Digital Access V2.0 which would be available from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408”.

"Aim of this blog post is to demonstrate the abilities of custom entities in RAP and create a Fiori application with composition relationships highlighting multiple challenges and learnings".

 

It’s a standard guideline for developing Fiori apps with SAP S/4HANA recommended practices like RAP as the backend implementation, OData as the Service provider and Fiori as UX

In RAP there are multiple approaches available to build a Fiori app and it is recommended to consider the below approaches using RAP entities.

New/Greenfield Implementation

Implementation to be built from scratch

RAP BO with Managed Implementation

Brownfield Implementation

Implementation to be reused from legacy

RAP BO with Unmanaged Implementation

The above approaches are good for below cases.

  • Transactional application with CRUD operations
  • Data model with Parent child relationship between the entities
  • With features which corresponds to Behavior definition & implementation
  • Predefined data sources for defining the RAP entities.

But we cannot rely on RAP view entities when we wanted an app with the below scenarios:

  • Data model with unrelated entities
  • Complete custom business logic for each dataset
  • Data is mostly derived out of application layer logic since there is no predefined data source available.
  • Display only app where Behavior definition is not required.

In these scenarios, RAP Custom entities would be beneficial.

 

Use case:

We have a Fiori app which is already available with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402 release called License Compliance Digital Access and we had a motivation to create a successor app with new functionalities.

Our motivation to create a new successor app

  • Requires incompatible changes to existing UI.
  • The existing app doesn’t support custom date range for selection of license entitlements.
  • Performance degradation over the period due to creation of large volume of digital access documents which couldn’t be improved.

So, we have come across a point where we must build our app with the latest recommended guidelines as per SAP S/4HANA in terms of UX design, RAP modelling and OData V4.

So below is the way the app should be transformed.

Key Attributes

Existing App

Successor App

UI Template

Free Style

Fiori Element

UI Technology

SAP UI5

SAP Fiori

OData Version

OData V2

OData V4

Backend Implementation

SEGW Classes

RAP Custom Entities

 

Overview of License Compliance Digital Access V2.0

Displays the consumption of Digital Access Licenses by Customer.

Key Features

  • View details about the total license entitlement, Usage of the acquired licenses for a given period.
  • View details about individual licenses
  • View details about the count, weighted count, and total weighted count of document items for each document type
  • View active and deleted instances of metered data for a document created on a particular date.


Access License Compliance Digital Access V2.0 in Fiori Apps Library

*Note: The blog is relevant for SAP S/4HANA OP 2022 or higher, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, SAP BTP, ABAP Environment and currently for the old app since new app would be available from SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408. Check out System Landscapes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for additional details System Landscapes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud


Now we will understand the technical aspects of the app along with the challenges / learnings.

Unmanaged Query - Custom Entities

Architecture

 

*Diagram illustrating the design of custom entities

 

 

*Diagram illustrating RAP BO custom entities hierarchy

 

  • The data model for an unmanaged query can be defined in a CDS custom entity. A custom entity defines the structure of the data returned by the query. This is done using CDS syntax in a CDS data definition (DDLS).
  • The data source for a CDS custom entity does not have an SQL view to select data from a database. Instead, the custom entity specifies an ABAP class that implements the query.

 

 

@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_OVERVIEW'​

 

 

          The above annotation is used to reference the query implementation class in the data definition of the CDS custom entity.

  • The ABAP annotation is evaluated when the unmanaged query is executed whereby the query implementation class is called to perform the query. For every query request on the CDS custom entity, the implementation class is instantiated. The class instance is never cached.
  • In a Business Object a custom entity can be an entity, for example a root, a parent, or a child entity using root and composition relationships. Custom entities may also be used as targets in the definition of associations and define associations as a source.
  • Access control needs to be implemented manually in the query implementation class to ensure that only those records are returned for which the user is allowed to access. You cannot use an access control object (DCL) for a custom entity.
  • Interface IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER - Unmanaged queries are implemented in ABAP classes. The query implementation class implements a predefined ABAP interface (IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER) to ensure that the required basic OData support is enabled.
    • Method select - When implementing the query, you must know that the implementation class only offers one method which is called “select”. The method select must be implemented in custom entity scenarios. It replaces the SQL-SELECT of a CDS view to retrieve and return data
    • The select imports an interface instance for the request data and one for the response data:
      • Interface IF_RAP_QUERY_REQUEST
      • Interface IF_RAP_QUERY_RESPONSE

 

Important points to consider while implementing a custom entity.

  • Initially, the code checks if the data is being requested or not to continue for further execution which is checked with below method call

 

 

io_request->is_data_requested( )

 

 

  • It is important to note that you must return the number of entries found, even though if a single request is returned otherwise no data will be shown in the object page. This is done using the below statement

 

 

io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( lt_items ) ).

 

 

  • This number should not exceed the number of entries that have been requested by the client or, the number that has been enforced by the framework if no $top and $skip has been used.
  • The data both for the single read as well as for queries is returned as an internal table to the framework.

 

 

io_request->set_data( lt_items ).

 

 

 

 

Challenges/Learnings:

We had many challenges during the development of this app which we would like to highlight here.

1. Multiple views on a List Report with different entity sets.

We had a requirement to cater the design of old free style UI into the predefined Fiori template offered by SAP.

In this process we need to have two views in the Fiori list page which is not the default solution offered by Fiori standard template, we now have to go through a minor UI breakout in order to achieve this, we have also used concept of SelectionVariants, PresentationVariants, and SelectionPresentationVariants

 Please refer to the screenshot displaying two views on list report

 

Below are the steps to achieve this:

  1. First create an entity for the second view which is used as the data source, Use below code snippet in Main entity

 

 

.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
  id: 'id_Header',
  text: 'Header',
  selectionVariantQualifier: 'SVHeader',
  presentationVariantQualifier: 'PVHeader',
  qualifier: 'Header'
},
{
  id: 'id_Items',
  text: 'Items',
  selectionVariantQualifier: 'SVItems',
  presentationVariantQualifier: 'PVItems',
  qualifier: 'Items'
}]
@UI.selectionVariant: [
  {
    text: 'Header',
    qualifier: 'SVHeader'
  },
   {
    text: 'Items',
    qualifier: 'SVItems'
  }
]
@UI.presentationVariant: [
  {
    qualifier: 'PVHeader',
   visualizations: [{type: #AS_LINEITEM }]
  },
    {
    qualifier: 'PVItems',
   visualizations: [{type: #AS_LINEITEM }]
  }
]

 

 

     2. As a next step, Insert the below code snippet in the second entity which is to be displayed as another view

 

 

.selectionPresentationVariant: [
{
  id: 'id_Items',
  text: 'Items',
  selectionVariantQualifier: 'SVItems',
  presentationVariantQualifier: 'PVItems',
  qualifier: 'Items'
}]
@UI.selectionVariant: [
   {
    text: 'Items,
    qualifier: 'SVItems'
  }
]

@UI.presentationVariant: [
    {
    qualifier: 'PVItems',
   visualizations: [{type: #AS_LINEITEM }]
  }
]

 

 

        3. Now we navigate to UI project in BAS (Business Application Studio/Web IDE) and Insert/Modify the below code in the location Webapp/Manifest.json file in the targets section.

 

 

"targets": {
        "ItemsOverviewList": {
          "type": "Component",
          "id": "ItemsOverviewList",
          "name": "sap.fe.templates.ListReport",
          "options": {
            "settings": {
              "contextPath": "/ItemsOverview",
              "views": {
                "paths": [
                  {
                    "key": "id_Items",
                    "entitySet": "ItemDetail",
                    "annotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#Items"
                  },
                  {
                    "key": "id_Header",
                    "annotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#Header"
                  }
                ]
              }
​

 

 

2. Dynamic facet handling

This was a huge challenge where we had to call a page by navigation dynamically i.e., We must call Page3 from the navigation of Page 2 dynamically based on the document type selected on page 2. And we have almost 19 document types

We wanted to achieve this functionality without creating UI breakout and do with cds entities only.

Hence, we have come up with an idea to handle the facet dynamically during navigation from page2 to page 3, For this we followed the below steps:

  1. We have created Fields in Page 2 custom entity where we identify each field to each facet of the document type i.e., field IsSalesDocumentHidden represents facet of Sales Document, we then used this field to control the facet Dynamically as shown below.

 

 

// Fields to control Dynamic Facet Handling for 3rd Page
@EndUserText.label             : 'Sales Document Hidden'
@UI.hidden                     : true
IsSalesDocumentHidden;

 

 

     2. We have created Parent child composition for all the child’s for each document type of the entity like below

 

 

@ObjectModel.filter.enabled    : false
_SalesDocumentList             : composition [0..*] of C_SalesDocumentList;

 

 

     3. We have created facet for every document type which corresponds to its entity like the below code snippet

 

 

  // Dynamic facet handling based on Document type selection
  .facet                      : [

  // Sales Document
  {
      id                         : '_SalesDocumentList',
      purpose                    : #STANDARD,
      position                   : 20,
      label                      : 'Items',
      type                       : #LINEITEM_REFERENCE,
      hidden                     : #(IsSalesDocumentHidden),
      targetElement              : '_SalesDocumentList'
  }

 

 

     4. Now we navigate to the UI project in BAS, and we have defined the routing configuration in Manifest.json

           a. Routes (Please refer to attached file - Define Routes)

           b. Targets ((Please refer to attached file - Define Targets) 

     5. Now we have to implement the logic to handle the facet in the custom entity by populating the field corresponding to the document type (Please refer to attached file - Handle display facet in UI using flag indicator).

 

3. Handling of data between List and Object page by segregation of code in custom entity

 For each custom entity there is only one implementation class possible.

Now this custom entity has both List page and Object page.

So, we use filters to differentiate between list and object page.

When the navigation happens from the List to object page then we will set the keys as part of filters, we will assume it to be the object page and query the database with the keys or delete all the other entries expect the one matching the key, please find the code snipped for the same below

 

 

" Delete all other data in case of object page header.
IF lv_DocumentItemID IS NOT INITIAL.
 DELETE lt_Itemsoverview_data WHERE DocumentItemID NE lv_DocumentItemID.
ENDIF.

 

 

4. Adapting free style UI to one of the Fiori standard templates – DATA POINT Annotations

In the existing old app, we have data displayed in the header like the KPIs but whereas in the Fiori list object page it is not possible to show data in the header, so we made use of DATA POINT annotations to show the data as to meet the UX requirements in the old app, (please find code snippets of the same attached - Datapoint annotations)

5. We are the first team to implement metadata extension to the custom entities since it was only available from the current release.

 

 

Related Blogs:

You can find some of the related blogs for your reference below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-implement-a-custom-entity-in-the-abap-restful-programming-model/ba-p/13406280

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-abap-rap-custom-entities-with-compositions-relationship-in-a-fiori/ba-p/13577865

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-create-a-fiori-elements-app-for-a-rap-bo-with-transport-selection/ba-p/13562792

https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-rap-opensap/blob/main/week5/unit3.md

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-a4c-create-custom-entity..html

https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/b6b59e4a4c3548cf83ff9c3b955d3ba3

 

 

Conclusion:

In this blog post, we learned about end-to-end implementation of RAP based Fiori application with unmanaged query i.e., Custom entities

I'd want to express my gratitude to everyone who offered assistance to me in writing this content especially my development colleagues @Mukund_Anand_Sinha and @sachin_b 

Hope you had a great time reading through the blog. Please Like, Follow and Share if you are interested in learning more on this topic, Also Kindly share your feedback or thoughts in the comments section below
 

Sending Good Vibes!
Shiva

entry.title: How to Create and Edit Error Specific Messages in the Manage Substitution/Validation Apps
entry.content:
In this blog post, we'll explore how to create and edit error specific messages for validation rules, using your own parameters in the Manage Substitution/Validation apps. These apps are used across various sectors, including Joint Venture Accounting, Sales Accounting, Group Reporting, and more. I will also show you how to maintain custom error messages.

I will demonstrate how to do this using the Manage Substitution and Validation for Public Sector app (App ID: F7603). You will need the BPC Expert role assigned to your user.

First, let’s first look at how to create a rule.

Choose ‘Validation Rule’ as your rule type.

(Please note that error messages are only available for validations)

Select your Business Context and Event.

Choose ‘Create’ and fill out the ‘Preconditions’.

 

Maintaining custom error messages

You have the option of defining your own message class. This needs to be done upfront with developer extensibility. If you don’t have your own message class, you can use the standard message class FINRE_UI_STD. There, you can find a set of predefined messages with variables which need to be filled out by the customer.

The message 000, especially, allows a lot of flexibility in defining your own message text.

The parameters can be filled with constants, or field/functions.

Fill out the parameters with the variables you want to see in your error message, for example let’s use Cost Center for parameter 1, and Test for parameter 2, using constant.  

As you can see below, Cost Center and Test appear in your error message:

“Value for field Cost Center doesn’t comply with validation rule Test”.

 

Conclusion

Creating and editing validation rules with custom error messages in the Manage Substitution/Validation apps is a straightforward process that allows you to fine-tune your validations and error messages. By following these steps, you can ensure that your rules are executed according to your specific needs, making your workflow more efficient and user-friendly. Please let me know in the comments if you have any questions, I am happy to help.

entry.title: Offshore vs. Onshore Instance: China Subsidiary-based Rollout for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In today's interconnected world, Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems are the backbone of global business operations, seamlessly integrating key functions such as finance, supply chain management, and human resources. As companies expand their global footprint, choosing the right SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system instance strategy for the Chinese market becomes crucial for operational success.

Marco_Valencia and I aim to guide businesses in making an informed choice between offshore and onshore ERP instances for their China subsidiary-based rollout of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. We focus on critical aspects such as regulations, performance, and strategic alignment. However, given that regulations are continually evolving and may have varying implications based on the nature and sector of businesses, it is essential to seek specialized legal and technical advice to thoroughly understand Chinese regulations and decide on the best strategy. While SAP is not able to provide any legal advice, we aim to help you understand some of the strategies and implications you might encounter when making your decision.

Understanding SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition System Instances

To kick things off, let's define what SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system instances are. These are complete environments (a 3-system landscape that includes development, test, and production systems including the HANA database, etc.) where software and data are hosted and managed. When it comes to deploying these instances to cover business requirements for Chinese subsidiaries, businesses usually choose between offshore and onshore options.

Now, we will compare offshore and onshore deployments, highlighting some of the benefits and drawbacks. This will provide you with a point of reference for evaluating options to meet the requirements of your Chinese subsidiary in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Offshore Instances: Hosted in data centers located outside of China, companies often leverage existing global infrastructure to cover all group entities, including Chinese subsidiaries.

Offshore Instance – Central Single Instance (Public Cloud only) 

Figure 1 - In the example below, we show a company headquartered in the USA with two subsidiaries: one in Germany and another in China. The customer deployed a central single instance of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, following an offshore instance strategy for China & Germany based on a USA Data Center.

Benefits:

  • Cost-Effective: Significant savings by leveraging existing global IT infrastructure and economies of scale.
  • Standardization: Ensures consistent management and processes across the enterprise via corporate template-based deployment, facilitating easier cross-entity comparisons and benchmarking.
  • Seamless Governance: Centralized process ownership aids in maintaining uniformity and simplifies business process governance across entities, enabling more efficient global reporting.
  • Intercompany Process: Out-of-box configured business processes to support cross-company transactions, reducing reconciliation efforts and improving financial closing times.
  • Unified Reporting: Centralized data storage enables real-time, enterprise-wide reporting and analytics, providing a holistic view of the organization's performance with the ability to tailor reports for specific units.
  • Master Data Harmonization: Easier implementation of global data standards, ensuring consistency in master data across all entities and facilitating more accurate group-level analysis.

Drawbacks:

  • Potential Compliance Challenges: Potential changes in Chinese data residency laws can lead to legal complications or operational restrictions.
  • Potential Latency Issues: Potential latency issues can hinder real-time operations and business efficiency in China, affecting the timeliness of data for decision-making.
  • Data Transfer Restrictions: Cross-border data transfer limitations may impede real-time reporting and analytics capabilities for the Chinese operations.
  • Maintenance schedule: Offshore maintenance schedule (Upgrades, Hotfix Collections, etc.) can overlap with Chinese business hours.

Onshore Instances: Hosted within China, often through local providers, these instances are tailored specifically to meet Chinese regulations and business requirements.

Onshore Instance – Federated Strategy (Public Cloud only) 

Figure 2 - In the example below, we showcase a company headquartered in the USA, with two subsidiaries: one in Germany and another in China. The customer deployed a federated instance strategy of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, following an onshore instance strategy for China. This provides a single instance to manage the Chinese subsidiary based on a Chinese data center, while managing the rest of the countries with their one respective instance in the USA.

Benefits:

  • Regulatory Compliance: Ease of adhering to local Chinese legal requirements and data protection laws, reducing legal risks and potential penalties.
  • Enhanced Performance: Minimal latency ensures efficient system performance and user experience, enabling real-time data processing and reporting.
  • Localized Security: Better control with security measures tailored to local needs and practices, potentially reducing the risk of data breaches.
  • Operational Autonomy: Subsidiaries can operate independently, addressing specific requirements and needs efficiently, including local reporting standards.
  • Tailored Innovation: Align new innovations with localized business processes, allowing for agile adaptations to changes in the local Chinese market.
  • Tailored Analytics: Ability to develop and implement analytics tools specifically designed for the Chinese market and regulatory environment.
  • Minimized Disruption: Limited system downtime affects only impacted Chinese units, reducing disruptions to global operations and reporting. The onshore maintenance schedule, which includes upgrades and hotfix collections, is separate from other customer offshore operations.

Drawbacks:

  • Higher Costs: Increased deployment, operational and maintenance costs, including potential duplication of systems and analytics tools.
  • Integration Issues: Complexity in integrating with other SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems and potential cross-border data flow restrictions, complicating group-level reporting and analytics.
  • Fragmented Financial Planning: Financial consolidation might face complications due to the fragmented systems, requiring additional effort to ensure data consistency and accuracy.
  • Intercompany Process challenges: Intercompany processes may not be supported out-of-the-box, requiring additional custom integration among instances and potentially leading to reconciliation discrepancies.
  • Master Data Inconsistencies: Challenges in maintaining consistent master data across separate instances, potentially leading to discrepancies in reporting and analytics at the group level.
  • Reduced Global Visibility: Separate instances may limit real-time visibility into Chinese operations for global management, complicating decision-making and performance monitoring.

Key Considerations for Choosing Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition System Instance

Organizational Unit Set-up

Understanding the organizational unit design versus the deployment model (offshore and onshore) is important to analyze carefully, as simplifying organizational units can also simplify intercompany processes and aid in decision-making.

Business Scale and Growth

Small to medium enterprises (SMEs) might favor offshore instances for immediate operational efficiency and cost benefits, whereas larger corporations could consider a hybrid approach. This hybrid approach leverages both onshore and offshore systems to balance costs, performance, and compliance as they scale, and to comply with changing regulations.

Geopolitical and Economic Factors

Geopolitical tensions, such as trade policies and international relations, can heavily influence the decision. Emerging trade restrictions may suddenly make offshore instances more problematic. Staying agile and well-informed on these factors ensures better strategic alignment.

Vendor Capabilities

Assessing the ERP vendor’s infrastructure and support capabilities within China is crucial. A reputable vendor with established local support can ease deployment and maintenance burdens, ensuring smoother operations. For example, technology providers like SAP support customers' compliance efforts by delivering technical features and functionalities that help adhere to applicable laws. Executing an onshore instance strategy with SAP will require a new contract with SAP China; we advise you to check with your current account executive.

Practical Steps to Making the Decision 

Assess Your Current and Future Needs Conduct a thorough assessment of your needs, encompassing current operations, compliance requirements, and future growth projections. Engage key stakeholders from various departments to capture a comprehensive view.

Consult with Experts

While SAP maintains a strong focus on China Regulatory Compliance, their information should not substitute for legal or regulatory advice. Always seek independent legal and regulatory advice tailored to your specific needs. Consult with ERP experts, including local and international consultants, for tailored advice. Their experience can uncover hidden challenges and opportunities.

Review Case Studies and Success Stories

Analyze case studies from similar businesses that have successfully managed ERP rollouts in China. These real-world examples offer valuable lessons and strategies for your decision-making process.

Conclusion

As of today, September 2024, most of our public cloud customers running operations in China currently follow the one global offshore instance approach, but several others are exploring the local onshore instance option. So choosing between offshore and onshore SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system instances for your China Subsidiary-based rollout is a multifaceted decision influenced by compliance, performance, and strategic factors. Onshore instances often offer better compliance and performance but come with higher costs and complexity. Offshore instances can be cost-effective but can face compliance and latency challenges. The best choice depends on your specific business needs, growth plans, and the geopolitical landscape. Consult with your ERP experts, run pilot tests, and thoroughly assess your requirements to create a customized solution that ensures both legal compliance and operational efficiency.

We would like to thank Jan Musil, Jens Fuchs, Khaled Musilhy , Janusz Smilek, Chi Song and Bert Schulze for their review and feedback on this blog.

Some sources for reference

Data Security Law of the People's Republic of China

Personal Information Protection Law of the People's Republic of China

China issues regulations on cross-border data flows

How to allow two affiliated companies to participate in a single intercompany process

How to transfer stock between different legal entities of a coporate group

Integration Architecture Guide for Cloud and Hybrid Landscapes

entry.title: Automating China VAT Invoice Process
entry.content:
China VAT Invoice Management, a product in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, provides end-to-end management for incoming and outgoing VAT Invoices. In the app Manage Incoming VAT Invoices, which is part of the incoming VAT invoice management, you can manually bind related documents, such as supplier invoice and accounting document, to a VAT invoice. However, you may prefer an automatic solution that the related documents are created along with the VAT invoice and the binding relationship between them is established simultaneously. To fulfill this requirement, SAP delivers the BAdI ‘FICN_TXI_MGMT_PROC_AFT_SAVE’ (refer to implementation guide)to automatically create, post supplier invoices, and do the binding via OData APIs.

Information: Typically, OData calls are used to interact with external systems. However, in this case, the OData APIs and the BAdI are in the same SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. The reason OData API is applied, rather than other APIs such as Supplier Invoice BO Interface (BOI), is that the BOI is only available for cloud development, not for key user apps. Additionally, for cloud development, a three-system architecture (DEV, QAS, PRD) is required, which can be prohibitive for some customers.

In this blog post, we will show you how to automate the process when a VAT invoice is created by OData API or is uploaded to the Manage Incoming VAT Invoices app.

You need to follow the two-part procedure consists of the following:

  1. Configuring communication management (for OData API)
  2. Implementing the BAdI

Part 1: Communication Management

To consume the Supplier Invoice OData API, you need to create the following for communication configuration:

  • 1 communication user
  • 1 communication system
  • 1 communication scenario for outbound services
  • 3 communication arrangements

In addition to creating the above communication scenario for outbound services, you also need 2 communication scenarios for inbound services. You can use either of the following ways to enable the communication scenarios for inbound services:

  • Reuse the communication scenarios pre-delivered by SAP: ‘SAP_COM_0057’ and ‘SAP_COM_0546’.
  • Create the communication scenarios on your own.

Note that the required authorization for calling the supplier invoice OData is automatically assigned to the communication user. If you don’t want to reuse the communication scenarios for inbound services, you must manually maintain the authorization objects when creating the communication scenarios. You can find more information here about communication management here and below is a schematic system diagram in the case. 

Note: The screenshots below are taken from an SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility system x6c as examples.

1.1 Communication User

Log into the SAP Fiori launchpad in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. Open the app Maintain Communication User and create a user.

1.2 Communication System

Open the app Communication System, create a communication system with the following information:

  • System ID
  • System Name
  • Host Name
  • Port
  • Users for Inbound Communication, Users for Outbound Communication (the user created in step 1.1)

As the communication happens in the same SAP S/4HANA Cloud instance, you only need one communication system.

1.3 Communication Scenarios

Scenario ‘SAP_COM_0057’ is used for calling the Supplier Invoice OData APIs from the outside.

Scenario ‘SAP_COM_0546’ is used for calling the incoming VAT Invoice OData APIs from the outside.

Open the app Custom Communication Scenarios, you take the steps below create and publish a scenario for outbound services:

  1. Maintain the required data.
  2. Under the “Outbound Services” tab, add services and URL paths for both supplier invoice and VAT invoice OData APIs.
  3. Publish the scenario.

1.4 Communication Arrangements

Open the app ‘Communication Arrangements’, create communication arrangements with the following information:

  • Scenario
  • Arrangement Name
  • Communication System (created in 1.2)
  • User Name (created in 1.1)

You create the arrangements for inbound calls with Supplier Invoice and VAT Invoice OData services using the scenarios “SAP_COM_0057” and “SAP_COM_0546” separately. The communication system and the service defined in the scenario constitute a complete service URL. With the username and the authentication method, the service can be securely consumed.

You create the arrangement for outbound call based on the scenario created for outbound services. The outbound service channel is established along with the arrangement. With the channel, http client can be initialized to call the active outbound services in the arrangement.

Part 2: BAdI Implementation 

Open the app Custom Logic, create a BAdI implementation for the BAdI “FICN_TXI_MGMT_PROC_AFT_SAVE” using steps below: 

         2.1 Initialize the http client for calling supplier invoice OData service.

 DATA(lo_client) = cl_ble_http_client=>create(

communication_scenario      = '<YY1_Z_CALLSI_CHUGE>'

outbound_service                 = '<YY1_SUPPLIERINVOICESERVICE_REST'> ).

“ Here you use the scenario created for outbound service as the client is designed to call outbound service requests”.  

          2.2 Get the CSRF token.

 DATA(lv_request) = cl_ble_http_request=>create(

        )->set_method(

            if_ble_http_request=>co_method-get

        )->set_header_parameter(

            EXPORTING

                name = 'Content-Type'

                value = 'application/json; charset=utf-8'

        )->set_header_parameter(

            EXPORTING

                name = 'x-csrf-token'

                value = 'fetch' ).

DATA(response) = lo_client->send( lv_request ).

DATA(lv_token) = response->get_header_parameter( 'x-csrf-token' ).

2.3 Initialize the http client for calling VAT Invoice OData service.

DATA(lo_client_vat) = cl_ble_http_client=>create(

communication_scenario = '<YY1_Z_CALLSI_CHUGE>'

outbound_service            = '<YY1_CHINA_VAT_INVOICE_REST>' ).

2.4 Get the detailed China VAT invoice.

concatenate '/A_CN_TaxInputInvoice(guid''' <lv_invc_uuid>  ''')' into data(lv_rsc_ext).

DATA(lv_request_get) = cl_ble_http_request=>create(

              )->set_method( if_ble_http_request=>co_method-get

              )->set_resource_extension( lv_rsc_ext )->set_header_parameter(

              EXPORTING

                  name = 'Accept'

                         value = 'application/json' ).

data(lv_invc_response) = lo_client_vat->send( lv_request_get ).

              “ Note that <lv_invc_uuid> is derived from the BADI interface.

2.5 Prepare the payload for creating a supplier invoice.

DATA lv_payload type string.

concatenate '{ "CompanyCode": <lv_inv_cc>, "DocumentDate": "/Date(1722150000000)/", "PostingDate": "/Date(1722150000000)/", "DocumentCurrency": "CNY","InvoicingParty": "13100088",'

' "InvoiceGrossAmount": <lv_inv_amount>, "TaxDeterminationDate": "/Date(1722150000000)/","SupplierInvoiceIDByInvcgParty":"10000","to_SupplierInvoiceItemGLAcct":[{'

'"SupplierInvoiceItem":"1","GLAccount":"12610000","SupplierInvoiceItemAmount":"100", "DocumentCurrency":"CNY", "DebitCreditCode":"S","TaxCode":"J3","TaxBaseAmountInTransCrcy":"100" } ]} '

 into lv_payload.

 “ Note that the company code <lv_inv_cc> and the invoice gross amount <lv_inv_amount> are derived

 " from the China VAT Invoice OData response ‘lv_invc_response’.                                                   

2.6 Set resource extension and send the request.

DATA(lv_request_post) = cl_ble_http_request=>create(

              )->set_method( if_ble_http_request=>co_method-post

              )->set_body( lv_payload )->set_resource_extension( '/A_SupplierInvoice'

            )->set_header_parameter(

                EXPORTING

                    name = 'Content-Type'

                    value = 'application/json; charset=utf-8'

            )->set_header_parameter(

                EXPORTING

                    name = 'x-csrf-token'

                    value = lv_token ).

DATA(response_post) = lo_client->send( lv_request_post ).

" Note that you must maintain the resource extension here. If you add the entity type when creating the scenario, the http call will fail.

2.7 Bind the supplier invoice and the VAT invoice.

concatenate '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="iso-8859-1" ?> <asx:abap version = "1.0" xmlns:asx = "http://www.sap.com/abapxml">'

'<asx:values> <OBJECTKEY> <GJAHR>2024</GJAHR> <BELNR> <lv_si_nmbr> </BELNR> </OBJECTKEY> </asx:values> </asx:abap>' into lv_xml.

data(lo_add_ref) = cl_txi_input_invc_add_ref=>get_instance( ).

ls_ref_doc-cn_taxinvcbindgobjkeyformat = 'X'.

      ls_ref_doc-cn_taxinvcbindgsrcebusobjkey = lv_xml.

      ls_ref_doc-cn_taxinvcbindgsrcebusobjtype = 'MIRO'.

      APPEND ls_ref_doc TO lt_ref_doc.

      TRY.

      lv_success = lo_add_ref->add_ref_docs(

             iv_cn_taxinvccode = <lv_inv_code>

             iv_cn_taxinvcnmbr = <lv_inv_nmbr>

             it_invc_ref_doc   = lt_ref_doc

              ).

CATCH cx_txi_input_mgmt into data(error).

              data(lv_text) = error->get_text( ).

              cl_ble_trace_writer=>write_info_message( lv_text ).

        ENDTRY.

        “ Note that invoice code <lv_inv_code> and invoice number <lv_inv_nmbr> can be derived from the BAdI interface.

        " Supplier invoice number <lv_si_nmbr> can be derived from the supplier invoice OData response lv_request_post.

Feel free to leave a comment on this post below. We’re looking forward to your feedback.

entry.title: 📢 Transition from TS410 to Cloud-Based IE2EE
entry.content:
SAP University Alliances member institutions have long offered SAP certification to highlight students' SAP expertise. The "Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA (TS410)" course will retire on June 30, 2025 and be replaced by "End-to-End Business Processes for the Intelligent Enterprise (IE2EE)" with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, which is now available. SAP University Alliances will provide next steps for users of TS410. 

📢 For new certifications, start with the cloud-based course

entry.title: Cloud ERP two-tier Confluence Event Highlights – Blog collection
entry.content:
We are excited to share highlights of Cloud ERP Two-tier Confluence Event through this blog series.

Reflecting on the event, we delved into an overview, master data integration, various sales office scenarios and recent innovations. This blog series aims to help users better understand Two-tier ERP with  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  

Two-tier ERP addresses groups of companies including headquarters and subsidiaries. It comprises a headquarters ERP with high landscape complexity and being highly customized, and a subsidiaries ERP to support quick scale at lower costs with low landscape complexity. Both headquarters and subsidiaries ERP are integrated in a hybrid landscape with suitable implementation and process optimizations

Check below blog links to know updates from each of the areas

  • Two-tier ERP Overview
  • Two-tier ERP Master Data Replication Options
  • Two-tier ERP Transactional Data Replication options 
  • Sales Office scenarios in Two-tier ERP 
  • Two-tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

To know more click on " What is two-tier ERP? " page on sap.com

You can also reach out two-tier ERP community for more clarifications

Stay tuned for in-depth posts on each topic

entry.title: 基于MTO产品的公司间销售流程介绍
entry.content:
业务需求

客户业务流程是基于按订单生产的公司间销售业务.

流程介绍

1.创建公司间销售订单，订单类型OR

选择订单抬头-开票凭证页签，Tax Departure C/R选择和售达方对应的国家

选择订单行项目-开票计划

检查公司间价格条件类型

2.创建完销售订单后，可以参照标准流程BJE来完成MTO的生产流程，流程介绍可以在SAP for Me的ALM中下载

完成BJE以下流程：

  • 运行MRP，APP：Monitor External Requirements或者Monitor Material Coverage - Net / Individual Segments 
  • 将运行MRP后的计划订单转成生产订单 APP：Monitor Material Coverage - Net / Individual Segments 并且Release
  • 拣配组件, APP：Pick Components for Production Orders
  • 确定工艺流程活动，APP：Confirm Production Operation 
  • 对工单收货，APP：Post Goods Receipt for Production Order

3.创建交货单，对交货单进行拣配和发货过账

4.创建客户开票，凭证类型F2，APP:Create Billing Documents或者VF01，基于销售订单开票（对应第一步的开票计划）

5.再创建完客户开票F2凭证后，才能开公司间开票IV凭证，基于交货单创建

检查打印中的IDOC是否成功

6.查看通过IDOC自动创建的供应商发票财务日记账, APP: Manage journal entries

系统配置

完成1HO Set-up instructions中所有配置点

关于IDOC，有几个点需要注意：

1.OPD, 少了这步开票凭证打印中的IDOC就出不来

2.Make setting for posting, 字段Tax Jur.Code Transf要打勾，不然IDOC会报税相关的错

3.Assign G/L Accounts, 选择MTO产品对应的评估类进行配置，比如7920

4.Assign Tax Codes, 税码也要注意

欢迎大家使用尝试，有问题可以沟通

 

感谢观看

entry.title: Partner Delivery Manager (PDM) Newsletter GROW with SAP - October 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Welcome to the PDM Newsletter GROW with SAP Blog

We're excited to introduce our monthly newsletter blog, your go-to resource for staying connected with the latest innovations, insights, and trends in the GROW with SAP ecosystem. Each edition is a selection of exclusive expert guidance, inspiring stories, and actionable content designed to help you enhance your delivery management skills and develop your partner delivery practice to stay ahead in the ever-evolving world of SAP.

We encourage you to become an active member of our vibrant community by joining our partner groups, and in particular for cloud ERP our Implementation knowledge, experience sharing and  Q&A group: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ct-p/s4hana-cloud-public 

Let's connect and grow together!

Kind regards / Cordialement / Mit freundlichen Grüßen,

Sebastian Sauer

Partner Delivery Manager GROW with SAP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud) - EMEA Region

SAP Ecosystem Delivery Success

 

Note:  Links require access to SAP PartnerEdge or SAP Learning Hub

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition    #GROW with SAP#                                                                                                                                                             

Top Highlights

Overview

       Register/Access

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Enterprise Cloud Services – Partner Summit 2024 (EMEA/MEE)

SAP Enterprise Cloud Services Partner Summit 2024' is a virtual summit to empower our SAP PartnerEdge, Sell and SAP PartnerEdge, Service partners. It will be scenario-based training comprised of some of the topics listed below.

            Register

 

  • Session: 15th October 2024
    09:00 AM to 12:00 PM CET

 

  • Session: 16th  October 2024
    09:00 AM to 11:30 PM CET

 

 

Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release

The second major release of the year 2024 is here! The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release is filled with innovations that boost your productivity and support your everyday work life.

We are thrilled to give you further insights on our fully-fledged cloud ERP, supporting your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise in this blog post.

 

          Access

 

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Essentials for Finance

Welcome to the implementation essentials event by expertise services on the topic of finance. This session is to support project teams preparing for, or involved in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations.

This is a 3-day event, each day is 2.5 hours, and will include the following topic areas related to the finance line of business:

  • Key finance implementation decisions will be discussed (ledger/parallel accounting/chart of accounts/fiscal year variant/currencies)
  • Update to date major innovations included (financial accounting/management accounting/financial operations/cash/group reporting/closing/AI etc.)
  • Presenters will share implementation experience and lessons learned

         Register

  • Session 1: 15th October 2024, 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM Europe/Amsterdam
  • Session 2: 16th October 2024, 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM Europe/Amsterdam
  • Session 3: 17th October 2024, 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM Europe/Amsterdam

 

Partner Learning Series: Optimizing SAP Portfolio in the Public Sector

The Partner Learning Series is tailored to elevate the learning experiences of our partners seeking to maximize the use of the SAP Portfolio in the Public Sector. Our on-demand sessions, each lasting approximately 20 minutes, provide a deeper understanding of transitioning government customers to the Cloud in collaboration with experienced industry experts. You'll have the flexibility to learn at your own pace about SAP's Strategy, priorities, and trends, including SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and cloud deployment options.

The series delves into SAP S/4HANA Cloud, RISE with SAP, GROW with SAP, and SAP Business AI within the Public Sector industry, with a focus on grants and funds management, SAP SuccessFactors HCM, sourcing and procurement, and citizen services. Join us as we explore new insights and strategies to drive success in the Public Sector.

        Access

 

 

 

 

 

 

  
       

Events and Webinars for Partners

Overview

    Register/Access

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Webinar: GROW with SAP with Concur Expense – deep-dive session

Discover the exciting opportunity we now have to offer the value of Concur Expense to our GROW with SAP premium customers. During this session we will cover:

  • GROW with SAP w/Concur entitlement & Concur partner programs
  • Concur Expense product overview and SAP S/4HANA w/Expense value prop
  • Integration and implementation
  • The opportunity and next steps

 

     Replay

  • Session 1: 3rd October 2024, 09:00 AM - 10:00 AM Europe/Berlin
  • Session 2: 3rd October 2024, 5:00 PM - 6:00 PM Europe/Berlin

 

 

Webinar: Explore GROW with SAP entitlements - SAP Analytics Cloud for planning

SAP Analytics Cloud is the one planning solution combining business intelligence, augmented analytics, and built-in collaboration tools for everyone to work together and make faster, confident decisions. Learn more about them in this session

    Replay

  • Session 1: 4th October 2024,  12:00 AM - 13:00 AM SGT

 

 

Webinar: SAP Supply Chain Management for GROW with SAP

This session will provide you with an overview of GROW with SAP, and how SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition helps companies with the embedded Supply Chain Management capabilities. The focus of the session is to introduce the complementing solutions from the SAP Supply Chain Management portfolio. These solutions integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and provide a perfect upsell opportunity for customers who have specific requirements in Supply Chain Management processes. You will learn about how to position these solutions in the GROW with SAP context, and about customer success stories.

     Replay

 

  • Session 1: 7th October 2024, 4:00 PM GMT - 5:00 PM Europe/Berlin

 

 

 

 

Webinar: Introduction to SAP Information Lifecycle Management (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Hot Topic Series)

During this session our experts will provide insights on the "Why" and "How" of SAP Information Lifecycle Management. We will discuss possible use-cases and provide an overview of features and functions. Additionally, we will showcase the differences between SAP ILM in an SAP S/4HANA on-prem or private cloud deployment vs. an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition deployment.  For example, you will learn about the SAP ILM advisor which provides guidance for the data volume aspect of SAP ILM and get to know about archive file storage infrastructure. 

 

    Replay

  • Session 1: 8th October 2024,  10:00 AM - 11:00 AM Europe/Amsterdam

 

 

 

 

 

 

Webinar: Expand the value of GROW with SAP with add-on packages for chief financial officer, chief supply chain officer and chief human resources officer

In this session we will review currently offered GROW with SAP packages in financial management, digital supply chain and human resources. We will update you on the changes since the packages were launched earlier this year. You will also have an opportunity to ask questions.

     Replay

  • Session: 9th October 2024, 09:00 AM - 10:30 AM CET

 

 

 

Webinar: SAP Project and Resource Management for GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (deep dive)

After the training you will know how SAP Project and Resource Management integrates with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Professional Services scenario. How project staffing is done, which apps are provided and the key capabilities. Further you will learn which integration scenarios are available and how to proceed to setup your integration scenario..

       Replay

  • Session 1: 9th October 2024, 02:30 PM - 05:00 PM Asia/Singapore
 

 

 

Webinar: Explore GROW with SAP Entitlements – SAP Ariba Buying, SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

GROW with SAP entitlements offer support for customers looking to quickly and predictably adopt cloud ERP with continuous innovation. SAP Ariba Buying, base edition creates a smart and simple purchasing experience that increases user engagement. SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management on SAP Business Technology Platform is seamlessly integrated to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and provides a single point of access and visibility to supplier invoices from multiple connected systems. Learn more about them in this session

 

      Replay

  • Session 1: 9th October 2024, 12:00 PM - 01:00 PM Asia/Singapore

 

 

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Integration & Extensibility Fundamentals

In this interactive webinar, you will gain understanding on aspects & capabilities of Integration & Extensibility within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This webinar will start with an introduction to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud expertise services community, outlining the importance Integration & Extensibility in SAP Activate methodology, integration types and extensibility types – followed with an opportunity to interact and raise questions.

       Register

  • Session 1: 28th October 2024, 04:00 PM CET - 05:30 PM CET

 

 

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Essentials for Sourcing & Procurement 

Welcome to the Implementation Essentials event by Expertise Services on the topic of Sourcing & Procurement. This session is to support Partner project teams preparing for or involved in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations.

       Register

  • Session 1: 23rd October 2024, 3:00 PM - 5:00 PM Europe/Amsterdam
  • Session 2: 24th October 2024, 3:00 PM - 5:00 PM Europe/Amsterdam

 

 

Transformation with S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Overview

    Register/Access

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Find Release Information & Content with the Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Join us for an interactive virtual live session where we’ll walk you through the features and benefits of the Release Navigator. Discover how this tool provides a comprehensive view of upcoming releases, allowing you to easily plan and prepare for updates. You’ll also learn how to find release scheduling and maintenance windows within the release navigator, enabling you to better plan for and manage the deployment of updates.

 

       Access

 

 

Advanced Solution Bundling for Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Check out the latest blog post on our newsletter about the new features introduced in Solution Order Management with the rollout of the 2402 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This update supports the transition towards solution-based business models, offering a broad scope and range of customizable offerings. Find out more about how these business models drive competitive advantage and how the new solution bundles functionality in the 2402.3 release enables more efficient selling and processing of holistic solutions. Don't miss out on this insightful read.

 

       Access

 

 

Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

Don't miss out on the latest product updates for Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408! We are thrilled to introduce the integration of Returns Purchase Orders with Transportation Management, enabling you to seamlessly perform transportation planning, monitor execution, and settle freight costs for returns purchase orders as part of the Return to Supplier process (scope item BMK). Stay tuned for more details on these exciting features.

 

       Access

 

 

 

 

Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

Discover the latest innovations in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management with the release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. Our blog post outlines key topics, including reserving material using project demand (4RF), creating a project by copying an existing project using an API (1NT, 1YF), and integration with Project Plan Collaboration (6JN). Stay updated and explore how these innovations can elevate your project management experience.

 

       Access

 

 

 

 

 

Artificial Intelligence & Technology in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 

Check out the latest blog post on Artificial Intelligence and Technologies, highlighting the newest features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2408. Our release introduces a diverse range of AI-driven functionalities that are designed to elevate user experience, enhance operational efficiency, and foster better decision-making. With features such as Joule, AI-assisted financial insights, and intelligent resource matching, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release 2408 promises to optimize your business operations, making them more intuitive, accurate, and efficient. We extend our utmost appreciation to our dedicated developers for their hard work and commitment

 

        Access

 

 

SAP Recommendation: Assignment of Corporate Accounting Principle to Ledger

Check out our latest blog post, which provides guidance on the SAP Recommendation for assigning Corporate Accounting Principles to the Ledgers for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition (Public Cloud). The blog explains each scenario, outlines the future impacts if the recommendation is not followed, and discusses when it is possible to change the Corporate Accounting Principal assignment. Gain valuable insights into best practices for efficient Corporate Accounting Principal assignment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

         Access

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

       

 

Replay: SAP Cloud ALM – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Community Live

Join us for our community session hosted by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Expertise Services, where we dive into the topic of SAP Cloud ALM. This session is tailored for SAP partners looking to enhance their understanding of SAP Cloud ALM and optimize their implementation and operations experience with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition through this tool.

Participate in an interactive forum where you can explore key features, functionalities, and best practices of SAP Cloud ALM. Engage with SAP experts and fellow attendees to ask questions, share experiences, and gain valuable insights into how SAP Cloud ALM can streamline project management, monitor and operate innovation, and drive business success with SAP ERP public cloud. Don't miss out on this opportunity to expand your knowledge and network with industry professional.
 

           Replay

 

Other Updates

Overview

   Register/Access

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Explore Generative AI Hub with SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

SAP AI Core is a service in the SAP Business Technology Platform that helps customers and partners deploy and run AI models at scale without compromising data privacy and cost-effectiveness. It provides seamless integration with SAP solutions and any AI scenario can be easily realized using open-source frameworks

    Access

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Signavio Lab Space - Business Process Transformation Innovation

Discover the power of SAP Signavio Lab Space and learn how this innovation platform can contribute to a better SAP Signavio. This webcast will showcase how you can effectively engage with the platform and explore its experimental features. 

         Register

  • Session: 22th October 2024
    04:00 PM CEST

 

     

 

       
        

Partner Delivery Groups

Overview

Register/Access

 

 

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

This group focuses on providing enhanced learnings and periodic updates to the partner community with respect to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition product. Our goal is to facilitate collaboration with your peers and SAP subject matter experts.

 

Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

This private group is focused solely on helping you make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition projects successful. We are dedicated to your success and work to quickly scale the latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition product knowledge, and solution lifecycle experiences out to the wider community. We welcome your questions and feedback here and look forward to your active participation.

 

Access

SAP Business Technology Platform

Get up to speed with the latest content and activities in the SAP BTP Partner Group. Don’t miss out the upcoming SAP BTP Partner Community Call on September 20th. Get all the details, save your seat today and share with your network

Access

 

SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network

In the SAP Ariba and Business Network Delivery Group, we excited to share that we are welcoming our Test, Demonstration & Development Program team, led by Prasanth Rajan, to the SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Partner Delivery Group. This collaboration has allowed us to invite over 300 partner consultants who participate in the Test, Demonstration & Development Program. This initiative is key to enhancing our partner experiences and fostering stronger collaboration.

 

We are extending open invitations to our ISBN partners to ask their how-to questions, with a view to generate constructive and continuous learning in a growing partner group within SAP Community.

 

 

Access

SAP SuccessFactors HXM

SAP SuccessFactors is delivering value across multiple types of Artificial Intelligence (AI).

  • Generative AI: Accelerate HR processes and tasks by quickly generating or summarizing text across the SAP SuccessFactors HXM suite.
  • Conversational AI: Deliver a better employee experience while lowering personnel costs with a natural language processing chatbot.
  • Automation: Lower personnel costs while increasing data accuracy and accelerating repetitive core HR processes.
  • Deep Learning AI: Transform talent development and employee experience with personalized recommendations and deep insights.

 

Access

SAP Customer Experience & CRM

On regular cadences, we partner with internal Customer Experience & CRM subject matter experts to share best practices, new product information and thought leadership through delivery blogs in our partner delivery group. Our blogs are amplified through targeted communications and social media to help encourage engagement amongst partners and internal subject matter experts. Internal subject matter experts are always welcome to contribute blogs amongst any of our other solution partner delivery groups as well.

 

Access
